highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 eliminates hot-path allocations in HarfBuzz and SKShaper, and adds span-friendly SKColor parsing overloads.
highlights_body: Three allocation-free performance improvements land: HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse, SKFourByteTag.Parse, and SKShaper glyph results all drop per-call heap allocations. This line also adds ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads to SKColor parsing, removes the unnecessary unsafe modifier from GRContextOptions, and pulls in two rounds of upstream Skia m151 bug fixes.

bullets.lead: SKColor parsing is faster and span-friendly
bullets.detail: Parse and TryParse now use an allocation-free hex parser and add ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads, so callers can parse colors from larger buffers without slicing strings.

bullets.lead: GRContextOptions no longer requires unsafe context
bullets.detail: The unnecessary unsafe modifier has been removed from GRContextOptions, allowing projects to configure GPU contexts without enabling unsafe code.
Pulls in two rounds of Skia m151 upstream fixes, including the SubRunAllocator use-after-free that caused GPU text rendering instability.

bullets.lead: Allocation-free parsing in HarfBuzz and Skia tag types
bullets.detail: HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse and SKFourByteTag.Parse no longer allocate a char[4] on every call. SKShaper now reads glyph results through zero-copy spans, reducing GC pressure in text layout loops.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Added the cross-backend golden-image visual regression test harness
